ucs.title: [LLM] Akira (PUNK SPIDER / Howling Scorpius) VPN-to-RDP pivot via Cisco/SonicWall single-factor access
ucs.description: Hunts Akira's signature initial-access pattern: authentication to a Cisco ASA/AnyConnect or SonicWall SSL VPN appliance using stolen credentials on an account lacking MFA, followed within hours by interactive RDP to internal hosts from the same identity. CISA AA24-109A and Sophos/Arctic Wolf IR data both call this out as the dominant Akira foothold (CVE-2023-20269 brute-force / no-MFA local users on Cisco ASA, and SonicWall SSLVPN cred abuse).
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as vpn_time from datamodel=Authentication where (Authentication.app IN ("cisco:asa","cisco_anyconnect","sonicwall_sslvpn","sonicwall","ciscoasa") OR Authentication.signature_id IN ("%ASA-6-113019%","%ASA-6-716002%","%ASA-6-302013%")) AND Authentication.action=success by Authentication.user Authentication.src_ip Authentication.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` | rename src_ip as vpn_src_ip, dest as vpn_concentrator | eval vpn_user=lower(user) 
| join type=inner vpn_user [ | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as rdp_time values(All_Traffic.dest) as rdp_targets dc(All_Traffic.dest) as rdp_target_count from datamodel=Network_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port=3389 AND All_Traffic.action=allowed by All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.src | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | eval vpn_user=lower(user) | rename src as rdp_src ] 
| eval delta_min=round((rdp_time-vpn_time)/60,1) 
| where delta_min>=0 AND delta_min<=240 AND rdp_target_count>=2 
| table vpn_time rdp_time delta_min vpn_user vpn_src_ip vpn_concentrator rdp_src rdp_targets rdp_target_count
ucs.kql:
let suspectVpn = AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AppDisplayName has_any ("Cisco AnyConnect","SonicWall","GlobalProtect","Citrix Gateway") or ResourceDisplayName has_any ("VPN","AnyConnect")
| where ErrorCode == 0
| where MfaDetail == "" or AuthenticationRequirement == "singleFactorAuthentication" or ConditionalAccessStatus == "notApplied"
| project VpnTime=Timestamp, Account=tolower(AccountUpn), VpnSrcIp=IPAddress, VpnApp=AppDisplayName;
let rdpHops = IdentityLogonEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where LogonType == "RemoteInteractive" or Protocol =~ "Rdp"
| project RdpTime=Timestamp, Account=tolower(AccountName), DeviceName, RdpSrcIp=IPAddress;
suspectVpn
| join kind=inner rdpHops on Account
| extend DeltaMin = datetime_diff('minute', RdpTime, VpnTime)
| where DeltaMin between (0 .. 240)
| summarize FirstVpn=min(VpnTime), FirstRdp=min(RdpTime), RdpTargets=make_set(DeviceName,25), TargetCount=dcount(DeviceName), VpnSrcs=make_set(VpnSrcIp,5) by Account, VpnApp
| where TargetCount >= 2
| order by FirstRdp desc
ucs.rationale: Akira does not use bespoke malware loaders for initial access — the IR signature in nearly every published Akira intrusion (Sophos X-Ops, Arctic Wolf, CISA AA24-109A, Mandiant) is exactly: stolen creds against an internet-facing SSL VPN where the breached account had no MFA, then human-operated RDP fan-out within the first few hours. A generic 'unusual VPN logon' alert misses the actor; chaining V

ucs.title: [LLM] Akira pre-encryption staging chain: PowerShell/WMI shadow-copy wipe + RustDesk/AnyDesk drop + rclone exfil
ucs.description: Hunts the tightly-coupled tooling sequence Akira operators run on a beachhead/file-server in the 24-48h before detonation: PowerShell-based VSS removal via Get-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy | Remove-WmiObject, sideloaded RustDesk or AnyDesk for hands-on-keyboard, and rclone/WinSCP exfil to MEGA or a Tor-fronted host. This combination on one device is near-pathognomonic for Akira/Megazord (GOLD SAHARA).
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmds from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name="powershell.exe" OR Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmic.exe","vssadmin.exe")) AND ( (Processes.process="*Win32_Shadowcopy*" AND Processes.process="*Remove-WmiObject*") OR Processes.process="*Get-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy*" OR Processes.process="*shadowcopy delete*" OR Processes.process="*delete shadows /all /quiet*") by Processes.dest Processes.user _time 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | bin _time span=1d 
| join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("rustdesk.exe","AnyDesk.exe","rclone.exe","WinSCP.exe","PCHunter64.exe","PCHunter32.exe","masscan.exe","advanced_ip_scanner.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | stats values(process_name) as akira_tools dc(process_name) as tool_count by dest | where tool_count>=2 ] 
| join type=outer dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest_category="mega_nz" OR All_Traffic.dest IN ("*.mega.nz","*.mega.io") OR All_Traffic.app="rclone") by All_Traffic.src | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename src as dest | eval mega_seen=1 ] 
| table firstTime lastTime dest user cmds akira_tools tool_count mega_seen
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 2d;
let vssWipe = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmic.exe","vssadmin.exe"))
  and ( (ProcessCommandLine has "Win32_Shadowcopy" and ProcessCommandLine has "Remove-WmiObject")
     or ProcessCommandLine has "Get-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy"
     or ProcessCommandLine has_all ("shadows","delete")
     or ProcessCommandLine has "shadowcopy delete")
| project VssTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, VssCmd=ProcessCommandLine, VssUser=AccountName;
let akiraTools = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where FileName in~ ("rustdesk.exe","anydesk.exe","rclone.exe","winscp.exe","winscp.com","pchunter64.exe","pchunter32.exe","masscan.exe","advanced_ip_scanner.exe","netscan.exe","zemana.sys")
  or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("rclone copy","--config rclone.conf","mega:","--transfers ")
| summarize ToolFirst=min(Timestamp), Tools=make_set(FileName,15), ToolCount=dcount(FileName) by DeviceId, DeviceName
| where ToolCount >= 2 or Tools has_any ("rclone.exe","rustdesk.exe");
let exfilNet = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where RemoteUrl has_any ("mega.nz","mega.io","mega.co.nz") or RemoteIPType == "Public" and InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("rclone.exe","winscp.exe")
| summarize ExfilTime=min(Timestamp), ExfilDest=make_set(RemoteUrl,10) by DeviceId;
vssWipe
| join kind=inner akiraTools on DeviceId
| join kind=leftouter exfilNet on DeviceId
| project VssTime, DeviceName, VssUser, VssCmd, ToolFirst, Tools, ExfilTime, ExfilDest
| order by VssTime asc
ucs.rationale: Akira's playbook is unusually consistent across cases (Sophos X-Ops 'Crying Out Cloud', Arctic Wolf Labs Megazord report, CISA #StopRansomware: Akira). Operators specifically prefer the PowerShell+WMI shadow-copy removal one-liner (not vssadmin alone), pair it with RustDesk or AnyDesk for persistence even after IR begins, and stage exfil with rclone to MEGA. Each individual TTP is noisy alone; req
